summary: Strong Desolation Row vocal & fiery Little Red Rooster solo, solid second set
blurb: The show delivers a standout Desolation Row performance and a scorching Little Red Rooster solo from Brent. Estimated Prophet and the tight Drums/Space segment drive an energetic second set, while the overall flow remains cohesive despite occasional dips.
review: Feb 17, 1988 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captures one of the tighter nights of the run. Jerry’s voice is front‑and‑center on Desolation Row and Tennessee Jed, delivering soulful phrasing that many reviewers called “chills‑inducing.” Brent Mydland steals the spotlight with a blistering B‑3 solo on Little Red Rooster – described as “bursting out like a busted‑out jailbird” – and adds bluesy texture throughout Estimated Prophet. The second set builds momentum with Bertha, a crisp Drums/Space segment, and a confident Truckin' that rides tight dual‑drum interplay from Mickey and Bill, even if the set slides a bit during Black Peter. Overall the band stays locked in, offering a cohesive performance that stands out as a favorite from this Kaiser run despite a few uneven moments.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising Desolation Row vocal, Little Red Rooster solo and Estimated Prophet; one mixed review notes unevenness but still highlights strong moments. Consistent praise across sources warrants a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, featuring top‑notch Desolation Row vocal and the fiery Little Red Rooster solo.

key_highlights: Scorching Little Red Rooster B3 solo by Brent Mydland
Tight Drums/Space segment fueling an energetic second set

band_performance.Jerry: Soulful voice shines on Desolation Row and Tennessee Jed, delivering chills; some reviewers wished for more ballads but overall his lead work is strong.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation; crowd chant noted, though audience tape has bass slightly recessed and Box of Rain deemed merely passable.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal inflections on Desolation Row praised as a highlight; generally supportive rhythm guitar throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Blazing B‑3 solo on Little Red Rooster and bluesy keyboards on Estimated Prophet are repeatedly highlighted as the night’s high points.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are described as ambient, dreamy, and tight, especially during the Drums/Space segment that fuels the second set momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the tight, powerful drumming with Mickey; his work underpins the driving feel of Truckin' and the overall groove.
